# Executive Summary Template

Use this template to write the 300-word (±30 words) executive summary for the impact assessment. Fill in the bracketed placeholders with values from the assessment.

---

## Executive Summary: {{Initiative Name}}

{{Organization/Team}} currently {{1–2 sentence description of the business problem — the pain point, inefficiency, or risk that exists today. Be specific: cite the most impactful baseline metric.}}

**The proposal:** {{1–2 sentences describing the initiative — what will be done, what technology or process change is involved, and over what time horizon.}}

**Key performance impacts:**

- **{{KPI 1 name}}** improves from **{{baseline}}** to **{{target}}** — a **{{%}}** improvement that {{plain-language explanation of why this matters}}
- **{{KPI 2 name}}** moves from **{{baseline}}** to **{{target}}**, translating to **{{$ or hours equivalent}}** in annual savings
- **{{KPI 3 name}}** shifts from **{{baseline}}** to **{{target}}**, {{business impact in one clause}}

**Financial impact:** The initiative requires a **${{total investment}}** investment in Year 1 and is projected to return **${{total returns}}** annually — an ROI of **{{ROI %}}** with a payback period of **{{X}} months**. Over three years, the net present value is **${{3-year NPV}}** after applying a **20% confidence discount** to all projections.

**Improvement opportunities:** Across {{count}} identified opportunities, the initiative unlocks **${{total $ savings}}** in annual cost savings and frees **{{total hours}}** engineering hours per year. {{One analogy — e.g., "This is equivalent to adding X FTEs without hiring" or "enough capacity to deliver Y additional features per quarter."}}.

**Risk of inaction:** Maintaining the status quo exposes the organization to **${{cost of inaction}}** in annual costs that compound as the team grows. {{One sentence on competitive or operational risk.}} {{One sentence on talent risk.}}

**Recommendation:** {{One sentence call to action — approve, fund, pilot, or schedule a deeper review.}}

---

## Formatting Rules

- Total word count: **270–330 words**
- **Bold** all dollar amounts, percentages, and time figures
- No sentence longer than 25 words
- No jargon without plain-language translation
- Include exactly **one** comparison analogy
- End with a clear, single-sentence call to action
- Lead with the problem, not the solution
